findings.evidence: `rg -n "fundamental resolvent identity|theorem StronglyContinuousSemigroup.resolventRightInv"` shows line 874 uses that phrase, while lines 880-888 state only `lambda • S.resolvent ... x - S.generator ⟨S.resolvent ... x, ...⟩ = x`; the docstring itself says the left inverse/injectivity is not proved.
findings.fix: Retitle the docstring to say “right-inverse relation” or “right-inverse half” and avoid calling it the fundamental resolvent identity.
findings.issue: `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.resolventRightInv` is documented as “The fundamental resolvent identity,” but the theorem only states the right-inverse equation `(λI - A) R(λ) x = x`, not the usual resolvent identity or full inverse statement.

summary: One docstring still overstates a one-sided result as a resolvent identity. The public API is otherwise documented.
